name: Fresh Fig Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 1 quart Fresh, peeled pink figs (Celeste variety)
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush the peeled figs using a wooden spoon.
2. Scald the milk and cream together in a saucepan. Do not boil; skim any foam that forms.
3. In a separate bowl, beat the egg yolks and sugar until very light and creamy.
4. Gradually pour the hot milk mixture over the egg yolk mixture while continuously stirring to prevent curdling.
5. Stir in the stiffly beaten egg whites and cream sherry wine.
6. Allow the mixture to cool slightly before churning.
7. Once the ice cream is slightly frozen, stir in the crushed figs.
8. Transfer the mixture to an ice cream maker and continue churning until it reaches a firm texture.
9. Freeze for several hours to allow the ice cream to ripen before serving.
From Our Best Recipes by Lena E. Sturges, Food Editor. Birmingham, AL: Oxmoor House, Inc., 1970. Pg. 132. Library of Congress Catalog Number 70-140493.
